title: NEO 3.1 P1-S5 section 4.9 root-of-trust anchor schema
description: Schema for the section 4.9 ROOT_OF_TRUST_ANCHOR: a NAMED, host-held attestation stored OFF the governed surface (S:\NEO_backups\_neo_root_of_trust\...), outside NEO's write scope. It records host-held SHA-256 hashes of the artifacts that cannot pin themselves: lint_skills.ps1 (section 4.4a lint-self, otherwise circular) and .neo\gates\HUMAN_GATE_LEDGER.json (the RT4-R1 authority). verify_root_of_trust.ps1 consults this anchor (resolved from a FIXED host default; -Anchor override for trusted operators only, mirroring verify_app_slice's -HumanGateLedger) and fails closed on absent / malformed / wrong id / scope mismatch / protected-file drift, BEFORE lint's or AS4's verdict is trusted. PROVISIONAL-DEV honesty: in DEV the anchor is a plain file the session principal can write, so custody='provisional-dev' -> tamper-EVIDENT not tamper-PROOF. Genuine un-forgeability arrives only when Option A (S6) places it under an admin-owned ACL outside the principal's write scope (custody='host-anchored'); until then RT4-R1 PASS-WITH-AUTH is NOT trusted in PROD (the DEV->PROD push gate is the backstop).

anchor_id.description: The NAMED anchor identity. A verifier that loads an anchor whose anchor_id is not exactly this fails closed.

custody.description: 'provisional-dev' = a plain host file the session principal can still write (tamper-evident only) -> DEV. 'host-anchored' = under an admin-owned ACL outside the principal's write scope (Option A / S6) -> the only value a host-anchored trust claim may be honored against. NEVER 'host-anchored' in dev.

anchor_scope.description: Absolute path of the governed NEO root this anchor attests (e.g. '<NEO_ROOT>' for the dev anchor, 'S:\NEO' for the prod anchor). verify_root_of_trust.ps1 fails closed if this != the -NeoRoot being verified, so a dev anchor cannot be reused to attest prod (mirrors the app-tree-cannot-supply-authority precedent).

protected.description: One entry per artifact the anchor attests. Its on-disk SHA-256 (under anchor_scope) MUST equal sha256 or verification fails closed.

path.description: Repo-relative path (from anchor_scope) of the attested artifact.

role.description: Which circular-trust gap this entry closes: 'lint-self' (section 4.4a) or 'human-gate-ledger' (RT4-R1 authority anchoring).

sha256.description: Lowercase 64-hex host-held SHA-256 of the artifact at anchor-cut time.
